lecture_context.lecture_title: Lecture 17 - ConvNets and TreeRNNs for NLP

unit.name: Tree recursive neural networks and greedy parsing
unit.description: Motivates tree-structured composition from syntax and shows the simplest recursive neural network parser.
unit.summary: The lecture pivots from CNNs to tree recursive neural networks, motivated by the recursive, hierarchical structure of human language and the linguistic principle of compositionality [ts=2555s][ts=2877s]. Instead of composing representations along a flat sequence, the model builds phrase and sentence vectors over a parse tree so that meanings of larger constituents are derived from child constituents and a shared composition rule [ts=2935s][ts=3000s]. In the simplest version, two child vectors are concatenated, multiplied by a learned matrix with bias and nonlinearity to form a parent vector, and a second learned parameter vector scores whether that combination is a plausible constituent [ts=3020s][ts=3060s]. This makes greedy parsing possible: the model repeatedly merges the best adjacent pair, creating a binary tree and a sentence representation that can also be used for downstream tasks like sentiment analysis [ts=3080s][ts=3150s]. The instructor then explains the main limitation: a single shared composition matrix cannot model the differing semantic roles seen in adjective-noun versus verb-object combinations, motivating richer tree-composition mechanisms [ts=3208s][ts=3240s].

key_points.text: Tree recursive neural networks are motivated by the recursive hierarchical structure of natural language.
Compositionality says phrase meaning is determined by word meanings plus the rules that combine them.
A parent phrase vector can be built by concatenating two child vectors and applying a shared neural composition layer.
A second learned scoring function estimates whether two children form a plausible constituent.
Greedy parsing repeatedly merges the highest-scoring adjacent pair to produce a binary parse tree.
Using one shared composition matrix everywhere limits the model's ability to express varied semantic interactions.

kp_catalog.name: Compositional tree semantics
kp_catalog.description: Phrase meanings can be built compositionally over parse trees rather than only over flat token sequences.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because Compositional tree semantics provides prerequisite machinery that later units build on directly.

kp_catalog.name: Greedy tree recursive parser
kp_catalog.description: A recursive network can score and merge adjacent constituents greedily to build a binary parse and sentence representation.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because Greedy tree recursive parser captures the core idea that A recursive network can score and merge adjacent constituents greedily to build a binary parse and sentence representation.

kp_catalog.name: Recursive neural tensor network
kp_catalog.description: An RNTN uses tensor-mediated interactions to model richer phrase composition than a standard recursive neural network.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because Recursive neural tensor network synthesizes earlier ideas into a higher-level mechanism or takeaway.
